command: uv run python scripts/run_idea_foundry.py --corpus-jsonl data/hf_mirror/exploratory_d5_balanced/corpus.jsonl --candidates-output results/idea_foundry_candidates.json --probe-output results/IF1_repository_graph_signal_probe.json --experiment-id idea_foundry --seed 123

candidates.cheapest_falsifying_test: Scan D5 for import/test/doc edges, split leakage, and local edge resolution. Reject the pilot if resolvable edges are too sparse or cross split boundaries.

closest_primary_source_prior_art.exact_difference: GraphCodeBERT uses data-flow structure in encoder pre-training; IF1 tests repository-level import/test/doc edges inside a small causal decoder and keeps split-aware evaluation fixed.
closest_primary_source_prior_art.title: GraphCodeBERT: Pre-training Code Representations with Data Flow

candidates.expected_scaling: Bias lookup is O(edges in window) and should be negligible versus dense attention at 128-token context; preprocessing scales linearly with files.
candidates.likely_failure: D5 rows may not expose enough resolvable repository graph structure, and file-level edges may not land inside short 128-token windows often enough.
candidates.mechanism: Add typed repository-edge bias and optional edge prediction losses to a dense causal decoder. Edges are imports, exports, tests, docs, and same-symbol file links resolved inside a repository-aware split.
candidates.mechanism_occurrence_evidence: D5 rows contain repo, path, split, role, and source text fields that can support import/test/doc edge extraction without fetching external data.
candidates.name: Repository graph conditioned decoder
candidates.rocm_plan: Start with additive attention bias tensors in the existing explicit causal block; keep FP32 AdamW and finite masks before any fused-kernel work.

candidates.cheapest_falsifying_test: Chronological API-rename fixture: compare retrieval-only, fast-weight-only, and retrieval-plus-fast-weight answers after one verified update.

closest_primary_source_prior_art.exact_difference: Fast weights store recent sequence memories; IF2 makes the memory repository-scoped, signed, security-gated, and explicitly compared against retrieval and replay.
closest_primary_source_prior_art.title: Using Fast Weights to Attend to the Recent Past

equations: F_t = decay * F_{t-1} + eta * phi(k_t) phi(v_t)^T
candidates.expected_scaling: Associative matrix cost is O(d*r) per step for rank r; storage is bounded by explicit eviction and signed promotion policies.
candidates.likely_failure: Fast weights may memorize surface tokens without improving held-out repair or API reuse, and poisoning gates may reject most useful updates.
candidates.mechanism: Keep base weights fixed during a session and update a small associative fast-weight matrix from verified local examples. Promotion to persistent state requires regression and security gates.
candidates.mechanism_occurrence_evidence: Earlier E5 chronology fixtures already contain controlled update events and stale-document failures suitable for a one-update fast-weight proxy.
candidates.name: Signed fast-weight evolution scratchpad
candidates.rocm_plan: Prototype as small FP32/BF16 matrix multiplies beside the dense block, then benchmark whether HIP GEMM overhead beats retrieval latency.

candidates.cheapest_falsifying_test: Apply block-codebook reconstruction to T11c/T12 dense checkpoints and compare loss, bytes, and random-codebook controls.

closest_primary_source_prior_art.exact_difference: GPTQ quantizes existing weights; IF3 tests reconstructible block generation plus residual accounting and random-control codebooks.
closest_primary_source_prior_art.title: GPTQ: Accurate Post-Training Quantization for Generative Pre-trained Transformers

closest_primary_source_prior_art.exact_difference: Tensor train layers factorize tensors; IF3 uses audited learned block codebooks plus sparse/low-rank residuals for trained decoders.
closest_primary_source_prior_art.title: Tensorizing Neural Networks

equations: W_l,b ~= s_l,b * C[z_l,b] + U_l,b V_l,b^T + Sparse(R_l,b)
candidates.expected_scaling: Storage can fall below per-channel int8 only if codebook ids and residuals stay small; reconstruction adds O(block_size) unless cached.
candidates.likely_failure: Metadata and reconstruction caches may erase storage gains, or random codebook controls may match learned codebooks.
candidates.mechanism: Represent each trained weight block by a compact codebook id, scale, and small sparse residual. A deterministic generator reconstructs blocks from layer id, block coordinates, and codebook entries; all metadata is counted.
candidates.mechanism_occurrence_evidence: Existing quantization records already show uniform int8 is strong and int4 is weak, creating a clear compression baseline to beat.
candidates.name: Audited block-codebook weight generator
candidates.rocm_plan: First reconstruct into dense FP32/BF16 tensors for loss checks, then test a packed lookup/dequant kernel only if metadata-inclusive storage wins.

candidates.cheapest_falsifying_test: Build a tiny public-repo fixture with source, tests, docs, and API drift; verify file selection and signature accuracy beat source-only scoring.

closest_primary_source_prior_art.exact_difference: CodeT5 uses identifier-aware code/text objectives; IF4 requires executable test traces and source-doc drift negatives as paired signals.
closest_primary_source_prior_art.title: CodeT5: Identifier-aware Unified Pre-trained Encoder-Decoder Models for Code Understanding and Generation

equations: L = L_lm + lambda * InfoNCE(e_source, e_test_or_doc, negatives)
candidates.expected_scaling: Auxiliary pooling is linear in sampled paired spans; executable trace collection is the expensive offline step.
candidates.likely_failure: D5 may not include runnable environments, and weak filename/docstring pairs may add noise rather than useful task signal.
candidates.mechanism: Train with ordinary language modeling plus contrastive alignment between source spans, tests, docs, signatures, and observed executable traces.
candidates.mechanism_occurrence_evidence: D5 role labels include source, tests, README, documentation, docstrings, and changelogs, although roles overlap and timestamps are mostly absent.
candidates.name: Executable trace contrastive objective
candidates.rocm_plan: Share the dense-528 trunk, add small projection heads, and keep the auxiliary loss in FP32 until the baseline loss path is unchanged.

candidates.cheapest_falsifying_test: Measure bracket/comment/string-state prediction and causal span recovery on D5 before any full 50M-token training.

closest_primary_source_prior_art.exact_difference: Mamba makes SSM parameters input-selective; IF5 adds explicit code-syntax state features and tests source/span tasks rather than generic long-context claims.
closest_primary_source_prior_art.title: Mamba: Linear-Time Sequence Modeling with Selective State Spaces

equations: s_t = A(x_t, syntax_t) s_{t-1} + B(x_t, syntax_t) x_t
candidates.expected_scaling: Linear in sequence length for the mixer block; total model remains mostly transformer cost until more blocks are replaced.
candidates.likely_failure: At 128-token context the recurrent state may add overhead without enough long-range structure benefit.
candidates.mechanism: Replace one transformer block with a selective recurrent mixer whose state is explicitly modulated by indentation, bracket depth, comment state, and string-literal state.
candidates.mechanism_occurrence_evidence: D5 source files include syntax delimiters, comments, minified files, tests, and docs where explicit lexical state can be computed without labels.
candidates.name: Syntax-state recurrent mixer
candidates.rocm_plan: Start with eager PyTorch recurrent scan for correctness, then port the scan to a HIP-friendly chunked kernel only if the pilot beats dense loss/function.

candidates.cheapest_falsifying_test: Chronological multi-repo API reuse fixture with retrieval-only, dense, adapter, and specialist-lattice controls.

closest_primary_source_prior_art.exact_difference: LoRA trains low-rank adapters; IF6 adds repository-aware specialist lattices, consolidation gates, rollback, and retrieval/memory controls.
closest_primary_source_prior_art.title: LoRA: Low-Rank Adaptation of Large Language Models

closest_primary_source_prior_art.exact_difference: Switch routes tokens to experts; IF6 routes signed local deltas by repository/task and requires chronological continual-learning tests.
closest_primary_source_prior_art.title: Switch Transformers: Scaling to Trillion Parameter Models with Simple and Efficient Sparsity

equations: h' = h + sum_{k in topK(g(repo, task))} alpha_k Delta_k(h)
Delta_shared = argmin_D sum_k ||Delta_k - D c_k||_2 + beta * regressions
candidates.expected_scaling: Training scales with active specialists; inference overhead depends on top-k deltas and consolidation frequency.
candidates.likely_failure: Specialists may overfit repository identities, and routing/consolidation metadata may exceed any quality or storage gain.
candidates.mechanism: Keep a stable dense core and train small repository/language specialists, then periodically consolidate specialists into shared deltas only when held-out validation, security, and regression gates pass.
candidates.mechanism_occurrence_evidence: D5 has repository IDs, language labels, and role labels needed to define specialist scopes while preserving repository-aware splits.
candidates.name: Delta-consolidated specialist lattice
candidates.rocm_plan: Reuse the residual-adapter branch only as a control, then test specialist delta matmuls with explicit top-k dispatch and dense fallback.

notes: Six candidate mechanisms for the next research cycle. These are not quality claims until prototyped and evaluated.
